Server._comment: TCP port the bridge listens on. Override with `--port N` on the command line; CLI wins over this value.

Tracing._comment: Per-request audit JSON capture. OFF by default — turn on to debug a specific issue, then turn off (the JSON files contain full request and response bodies, including user prompts). Each request emits 4 files (inbound-req, inbound-resp, upstream-req, upstream-resp) sharing the same seq. Directory is relative to the .exe unless absolute.

Routing._comment: nginx-style locations. Each location is a self-contained closure: 'When' matches (leaves: Model/Effort/Header; composites: AllOf/AnyOf; top-level fields = implicit AllOf), 'Use' fully describes what to do (Model swap, EffortMap remapping, Headers Set/Remove). First-match-wins; no chain, no fall-through. Header Set/Remove restricted to a whitelist (anthropic-beta, Editor-Version, Editor-Plugin-Version, Copilot-Integration-Id, X-GitHub-Api-Version). Use 'anthropic-beta:pattern' in Remove for token-level strip with trailing '*' wildcard. NOTE: the opus-4.6/4.7 '+1M beta -> dedicated -1m variant' redirects were REMOVED in the 2026 model reconciliation. Copilot retired the claude-opus-4.6-1m and claude-opus-4.7-1m-internal ids (they 400 'not available for integrator'), and the opus-4.6/4.7 BASE ids now serve 1M context natively (probed: 600k+ token prompts return 200), so no redirect is needed -- the 1M beta passes through and the base model handles it, same as opus-4.8 and sonnet-5.
Routing._Locations_disabled_comment: DISABLED rule set (the config binder ignores any key not named exactly 'Locations', so a leading-underscore key is how this project 'comments out' JSON). To ENABLE: rename '_Locations_disabled' below to 'Locations' and rename the real 'Locations': [] above to something else (e.g. '_Locations_off'). Exactly one 'Locations' key may be active.

_Locations_disabled.Note: DISABLED: route Claude Code's claude-opus-4.8 traffic to Copilot gpt-5.5. EffortMap max->xhigh is REQUIRED here: Claude Code sends the Anthropic effort 'max', which gpt-5.5 (a Codex model) does not accept; mapping it at the routing layer is the operator's explicit intent (vs. T2's per-model DefaultEffort fallback, which would also land on xhigh but emit a 'not accepted' WARNING). NOTE: gpt-5.5 is a lossy fit for the Claude Code tool protocol and prone to the degenerate runaway documented in docs/gpt55-runaway-diagnosis.md; enable only for experimentation.

OutboundBeta._comment: anthropic-beta tokens stripped from EVERY outbound request, regardless of model. Patterns use the same trailing-* wildcard as Headers.Remove. advisor-tool-* is stripped because Copilot hard-400s it ('unsupported beta header(s): advisor-tool-2026-03-01') on every model. NOTE: structured-outputs-* was previously stripped too (a GCP Vertex org policy 400'd it), but a 2026-07 live probe found Copilot now accepts structured-outputs-2025-12-15 (200 on opus-4.8), so it is forwarded by default. A Vertex-backed tenant that still gets FAILED_PRECONDITION can add 'structured-outputs-*' back here.

UpstreamRetry._comment: Idempotent retry of transient upstream connection failures (net_http_client_execution_error / net_http_ssl_connection_failed) on POST /v1/messages. Only retries when the failure occurs BEFORE response headers arrive (request body never processed upstream, so re-sending is safe) — never mid-stream. MaxRetries=0 disables. Backoff: BaseDelayMs * BackoffMultiplier^(attempt-1), clamped to MaxDelayMs.

PoisonedContext._comment: Request-side observe-only scan for a poisoned transcript: detects a single tool stuck in a repeated-failure loop (its failed tool_results — is_error, or content starting with 'API Error:'/'Error:' — replayed by the client every turn). Structural, NOT phrase-matching: it keys on 'one tool name accumulating many failures', so it survives whatever error wording Copilot returns (region 400, quota, timeout, model-not-supported, ...). It never blocks or mutates the request — a heavily poisoned context can only be cured by the user compacting the session, and stripping the blocks would 400 upstream. The total failure count is recorded on the summary line (poisoned_tool_results=); when one tool's failures reach WarnThreshold the stage logs one WARNING naming that tool and telling the user to /compact. The bad output such a context can induce is caught separately by Detectors:RunawayGuard. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
PoisonedContext._WarnThreshold: Failed tool_results attributable to a SINGLE tool in one request, at/above which the 'compact your session' WARNING fires. Keyed on the worst single tool (not the grand total) so 'one tool looping' warns but 'a few unrelated one-off failures' does not. The gpt-5.5 runaway had one tool (Agent) fail 50x. <= 0 warns on any single failure.

Detectors._comment: Response-side detectors, run in one pass over the SSE stream. DoneFilter (drops the [DONE] terminator) is always on and has no config. The detector sections below are configurable.

ModelRewrite._comment: When true, rewrites `message.model` in the response (both buffered and the message_start SSE event) back to the model id the client originally requested — so Claude Code's session log and ccusage report claude-opus-4-8 instead of the back-end variant the bridge routed to. Turn off to let downstream tooling see the real back-end model.

ResponseLeakGuard._Signal: Error emitted to force a retry when a leaked tool call is detected. OverloadedError => overloaded_error (HTTP 529): retried, and Claude Code falls back to Sonnet after 3 in a row. ApiError => api_error (HTTP 500): retried without a model fallback.
ResponseLeakGuard._Signatures: Per-signature on/off switches. Turn one off to clear a false positive (e.g. you're discussing how <invoke> tool-use or a <task-notification> envelope works and the model echoes a sample) while leaving the rest of the guard active. The tripped signature and the exact key to disable it are named in both the retry error and the WARNING log. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing any switch.

ToolInputValidation._comment: Validates real tool_use input JSON against the declared tool schema after the tool block closes. Catches malformed tool arguments (for example missing required fields) before Claude Code turns them into an unrecoverable Invalid tool parameters failure. PreserveStream=true keeps TTFT and emits an SSE error on detection; false buffers the whole response and returns a real HTTP status. Read at startup - RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
ToolInputValidation._Signal: Error emitted on invalid tool input. OverloadedError => overloaded_error (HTTP 529): retryable. ApiError => api_error (HTTP 500).

RunawayGuard._comment: Volume circuit-breaker on the streamed response: aborts a degenerate-generation runaway (a model stuck emitting an unbounded stream of tiny fragments) before it hangs the client for minutes. Trips on either budget below. On a trip it emits the same retryable overloaded_error as ResponseLeakGuard and logs `runaway=true` on the request summary. Read at startup — RESTART copilot-bridge after changing a value.
RunawayGuard._MaxDeltaBytes: Cumulative content_block_delta payload bytes across the whole response. A real gpt-5.5 runaway hit ~17 MB; this is set above any legitimate single response so normal large outputs pass.
RunawayGuard._MaxDeltaCount: Per-content-block ceiling on content_block_delta events. The degenerate signature is one tool call emitting tens of thousands of tiny fragments (the runaway hit 27,643 in one block). <= 0 disables this check (byte budget still applies).
RunawayGuard._Signal: Error emitted on a trip. OverloadedError => overloaded_error (HTTP 529): retryable. ApiError => api_error (HTTP 500).
